Instructions

What is this portal for?

Researchers can use this portal to request data that is publicly available on Temu’s online platform in accordance with Article 40(12) of the Digital Services Act (DSA).

Who can make these data requests?

Only researchers who meet all of the following requirements can make these data requests.

Researchers must:

Complete theData access intake form

Be independent from commercial interests.

Be able to ensure the security and confidentiality of the data requested, including the protection of personal data.

Agree to the[Data Access Terms of Use](https://partner-eu.temu.com/protocol/temu_data_access_portal_terms_of_use_20240930.pdf)

The request must:

Describe the appropriate technical and organizational measures that are in place for ensuring the security and confidentiality of the data requested, including the protection of personal data.

Disclose the funding of the research.

Demonstrate that the access to the data and the time frames requested are necessary for, and proportionate to, the purposes of the research, and that the expected results of that research will contribute to:

The detection, identification, and understanding of potential systemic risks in the EU stemming from the design or functioning of Temu’s online platform and its related systems, including algorithmic systems, or from the use made of the online platform. (In accordance with Article 34(1) DSA.)

Such systemic risks may include:

(i) the dissemination of illegal content;

(ii) any actual or foreseeable negative effects for the exercise of fundamental rights;

(iii) any actual or foreseeable negative effects on civil discourse and electoral processes, and public security;

(iv) any actual or foreseeable negative effects in relation to gender-based violence, the protection of public health and minors and serious negative consequences to the person’s physical and mental well-being.

The assessment of the adequacy, efficiency, and impacts of the risk mitigation measures tailored to the systemic risks. (In accordance with Article 35 DSA.)

What we offer?

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Information on Products

This could include data points relating to products such as:

Product name and ID

Product category

Product price

Sales volume

Product rating

Number of user reviews, review content and when the review is posted

Country sites where the product or reviews are published

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Information on Traders

This includes data points relating to sellers/traders on the platform, such as:

Trader name

Trader storefront link

Business address

Product categories offered

Number of product listings currently available

Number of units sold

Trader rating

Number of followers

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Information on Illegal Content

This includes data points related to illegal listings or posts identified on the platform, such as:

Content type

Date of posting of the content

Type of restriction

Ground for decision

Application date of the decision

Territorial Scope of the decision

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Information on Trader Suspensions

This includes data on enforcement actions taken against traders, such as:

Punishment ID

Suspension category

Ground for decision

Application date of the decision

Removal date of the suspension

![](https://bstatic.kwcdn.com/partner/partner-master-web/static/media/icon5.c2de3396c76e402e517c.jpeg)

Information on Advertising

This includes data on the advertisements placed on Temu EU platform, accessible via our [Advertising Library](https://ads.temu.com/library) and retrievable through our [Advertising Library API](https://partner-eu.temu.com/documentation?menu_code=94af0dce5c054781807d5a0f45a2bd75). Data points include, for example:

Publication period

Advertiser name

Name of the person who paid for the advertisement

Impressions

Researchers may request access to additional publicly available data not listed above, provided their research contributes to detecting, identifying, or understanding systemic risks in the EU. Basic research API endpoints are available in the [API documentation](https://partner-eu.temu.com/documentation?menu_code=98501210a0cc465695e6d94e364bb83c), and dedicated APIs can be provided to qualified researchers upon such requests.

How to request access?

01

Submit Your Application:

Complete the application form with information about yourself, including your log-in credentials (i.e., email address, password and verification code), organization, and research.

02

Log in to the Partner Platform:

Use your log-in credentials to access the [Partner Platform](https://partner-eu.temu.com/login).

03

Await Approval:

Your submission will be reviewed, and you will be notified once a decision is made.

04

Access Data:

Upon approval, you will gain access to the requested data. Please follow the [Research Tools User Guide](https://partner-eu.temu.com/download/Research%20Tools%20User%20Guide.docx) to learn how to access and navigate the data.

Contact us

If you have any questions, please contact us at [partner@eur.temu.com](mailto:partner@eur.temu.com)

Data access intake form
